name: google-calendar
description: Reads Google Calendar calendar lists, events, settings, and access control rules through the Calendar API v3 using an OAuth2 refresh token. In architecture v2 this quarantine bundle dispatches live reads through a Tier-2 hook that delegates to the legacy connector until the wave 6 cutover.

risk.read: external Google Calendar API reads performed by the legacy connector via a Tier-2 hook

conformance.reason: Tier-2 hook-owned quarantine migration: internal/connectors/hooks/google-calendar delegates Check/Read to legacy internal/connectors/google-calendar, so conformance dynamic replay of the declarative shadow path is intentionally skipped. Static schema/docs/surface checks still run; behavior is covered by the hook unit tests and the existing legacy connector tests during the pre-cutover period.
